reviewed.by: Positive-case reel explaining the Nicene Creed (orthodoxy = the shared Christian core) + the richness of the text. On-screen quotes VERBATIM from verified Schaff PD (sources/creeds.json: nicene-creed-325 'God of God…' + nicene-creed-381 gospel/Spirit/hope), ellipses mark omissions; Jesus quote is a disclosed 325+381 composite. DUAL-CONSENSUS: apologia-orthodoxy CLEAN (quote fidelity confirmed; Trinity co-equal 'one God/three persons/fully God'; deity of Christ positive-case not Arian steelman; denominational neutrality held — filioque/procession clause omitted, only the uncontested co-glorification clause used; 'catholic = universal' gloss; 0 clarifier candidates) + apologia-neutrality CLEAN after one WEAK fix applied: voiceover 'the same words' → 'the same creed' (filioque is a dispute over the creed's words, so 'same words' overstated cross-tradition identity; scene-1 'still say together' kept, it's accurate). Positive-case only; Arianism named as the refuted error, never steelmanned.

voiceover: For seventeen hundred years, Christians of every tradition — Catholic, Orthodox, and Protestant — have confessed the same faith in the same creed: the Nicene Creed. That's what orthodoxy means. Not a denomination — the measuring line, the shared core every Christian holds. And every phrase is dense with meaning, forged to guard something precious. Of Jesus it says: God of God, Light of Light, very God of very God, begotten, not made, of one substance with the Father. So when some claimed the Son was only a creature, the church answered in one unbreakable phrase: begotten, not made. Then, in a single breath, the whole gospel: for our salvation he came down from heaven and was made man, was crucified under Pontius Pilate, and the third day he rose again, according to the Scriptures. The Spirit too — the Lord and Giver of life, who with the Father and the Son is worshiped and glorified. One God, three persons, fully divine. And it ends in hope: one holy, universal, apostolic church; the resurrection of the dead, and the life of the world to come. Not dry — dense. Every line worth knowing by heart.
source: sources/creeds.json (verified: nicene-creed-325 + nicene-creed-381, Schaff PD translation); library/early_church_trinity.html; docs/STATEMENT_OF_FAITH.md. All on-screen quotes verbatim Schaff (ellipses mark omissions); positive-case only.

sub.t: The Nicene Creed — the words Christians still say together.

scenes.kicker: IT DREW A LINE THAT HELD

lines.t: from heaven… and was made man;
the third day he rose again,
who with the Father and the Son
One God. Three persons. Fully God.
and the life of the world to come.”

sub.t: Read the Nicene Creed in full — see how much it holds.
